methods.rationale: Applies typical Series B → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.9x) to $50M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median

data.narrative:
Zeno Power is a Series B-stage space & aerospace company that has raised $50M to date. The valuation analysis draws from a comp universe of 5 public peers (RKLB, PL, LUNR, ASTS, SATL) and 8 private companies in the same sector and stage, with particular focus on three direct comparables: Aalyria ($1.3B), Hubble Network ($400M), and Sift ($274M).

Two valuation methods were applied. The stage-progression approach uses the $50M total raised as a proxy base valuation and applies a 1.9x Series B step-up multiplier, implying $95M. The peer-median method anchors to the three same-sector, same-stage comparables, which show a median valuation of $400M with a range of $274M to $1.3B. The public-comp anchoring method (median P/S of 59.8x across the 5 public tickers) is not applicable due to absence of target revenue data, which is typical for private firms at this stage.

The triangulated analysis across the two applicable methods supports a valuation range of $81M to $460M, with a median of $248M. The wide dispersion reflects high uncertainty inherent in early-stage space infrastructure valuations, driven by variance in the peer set itself (the $1.3B Aalyria valuation materially pulls the distribution upward). Key uncertainties include technology differentiation relative to high-valuation outliers and whether Zeno's capital efficiency and development trajectory more closely track the $274M–$400M middle band or the outlier tier.
